name: getSubAgent
description:
Spawns an isolated AI subagent to handle complex or multi-step tasks. Always fire-and-forget: the call returns immediately with job/project metadata, and the final result is delivered asynchronously.

[COMMON RULES]
1) Direct tools first: for simple one-step requests, use direct tools instead of getSubAgent.
2) Never write code inline in assistant text. Coding tasks must go through getSubAgent(type='develop').
3) One call per deliverable. If B depends on A, run A first and pass A's outputs into B.
4) Before every call, scan recent conversation for ARTIFACTS blocks, file/image/zip URLs, and pass relevant artifacts in task.
5) Do not expose projectId in normal user-facing text unless explicitly asked.
6) Nested calls: if you already run inside a project context, only spawn for truly independent subtasks requiring another tool palette.
7) Read your execution-level hint from system prompt addition (contains current type/depth and parent type/depth).

[RESUME ROUTING RULES]
- For RESUME jobs (projectId set), ALWAYS use type='resume'.
- Resume is LEVEL-1 ONLY: after a resume subagent has started, child spawns MUST NEVER use type='resume'.
- If a resume subagent delegates, it must choose a specialized non-resume type (research/media/develop/etc.) for child jobs.
- If the user asks for metadata already visible in context (project/job/status/links), answer directly and do not spawn.
- For resume calls, output only the tool call (no extra narration before/after), as required by system behavior.

[TYPE SECTIONS]
- resume: Dedicated level-1 resume/follow-up route for existing projectId. Never use for nested child spawns.
- generate: PDF and document assembly.
- media: Image generation/animation/video/token creation; include all sequential steps in one task.
- history: Only for retrieving specific facts from older session logs outside current context. Not for project resume.
- research: Live web search, webpage fetch, YouTube for current/general knowledge.
- develop: Any coding/build/fix/extend request.
- patch: Single targeted edit of one existing file URL; only callable from within develop flows.
- atlassian: Jira/Confluence operations.
- microsoft: Microsoft 365 / Graph API operations.
- orchestrate: Only for truly independent parallel parts needing different tool sets.
- test: Dummy subagent for pipeline testing without API costs.

task.description: Complete, self-contained task description written as a clear natural-language instruction. Must be non-empty and specific enough that the subagent can act on it without any additional context. Examples: 'Write a Node.js Express server that serves a REST API for a todo list with in-memory storage.' or 'Search the web for the current EUR/USD exchange rate and summarise the trend over the last 7 days.' For resume calls (projectId set), describe what the subagent should do next, e.g. 'Continue the previous task. Add unit tests for all exported functions.' — never use a placeholder like 'resume' or 'continue' alone. NEVER include a project ID (pattern: 01[A-Z0-9]{24}) in this field — project IDs belong in the projectId parameter only. ALWAYS scan the recent conversation before writing this field: look for ARTIFACTS blocks, file URLs, image URLs, or ZIP URLs from previous subagent responses. Include all relevant previously-produced artifacts in the task so the subagent can reuse them instead of regenerating.

type.description: Subagent type determining which tool palette is used. ALWAYS required. For NEW jobs (projectId empty), choose the type that matches the task. For RESUME jobs (projectId set), ALWAYS pass 'resume'. Resume is level-1 only: nested/child spawns must not use 'resume' and must pick a specialized non-resume type.

orchestration.description: Optional orchestration context that scopes this subagent's work. Prevents duplicate side effects when multiple subagents are coordinated.

channel_id.description: Optional: explicit channel ID overriding the type-based channel lookup.

projectId.description: Pass the project ID (26-char string, e.g. 01KNFCB4DBHTXBGC91HQZ1KC0A) to resume an existing project. The subagent continues from its full prior context. Pass an empty string for new jobs. Never embed the project ID in the task field.
